title: Why Can't Anyone Answer Questions About the Business? — Garrett Galow, WorkOS
summary: Garrett Galow from WorkOS presented Studio, an internal workspace that lets non-technical users ask business questions in natural language against Snowflake, Linear, and Notion, generating reusable widgets as declarative JavaScript. The system uses preflight sequencing for schema injection, explicit distrust of model knowledge, and query validation to ensure reliability for production use.
writeup: Garrett Galow of WorkOS presents Studio, an internal workspace that lets non-technical users query business data in natural language against Snowflake, Linear, and Notion, and produces reusable widgets as declarative JavaScript. The talk addresses a common gap: business questions that stall because answering them requires technical access. Concrete reliability techniques covered include preflight sequencing to inject schema context, explicit distrust of the model's built-in knowledge, and query validation before results are trusted in production.
